texto_base:
In many countries, walking out of a restaurant without leaving a tip feels almost unthinkable, regardless of the quality of service received. Tipping has become so deeply embedded in dining culture that most customers rarely stop to question why servers, unlike nearly every other profession, depend on customer generosity rather than a guaranteed wage to make a living. Yet a growing number of restaurant owners, economists, and even servers themselves are beginning to argue that this system deserves far more scrutiny than it typically receives.

The most obvious problem with tipping is its sheer unpredictability. A server's income can swing dramatically from one night to the next based on factors that have little to do with the quality of service provided: a slow Tuesday, a large table that tips poorly, or simply an unlucky assigned section of the restaurant. This volatility makes it extraordinarily difficult for workers to budget reliably, plan for emergencies, or qualify for loans and housing that require proof of stable income.

There is also considerable evidence that tipping systems tend to reflect and reinforce bias rather than genuine service quality. Numerous studies have found that customers tip based partly on factors entirely unrelated to service, including a server's race, gender, and even attractiveness. A system explicitly designed to reward good service ends up, in practice, distributing income according to criteria that have nothing to do with merit at all.

Defenders of tipping often argue that it motivates servers to work harder and provide better service than a flat wage ever could. This claim sounds intuitive, but the research supporting it is remarkably thin; several studies have found only a weak relationship between the quality of service and the size of the tip actually left. If tipping genuinely functioned as a precise reward for excellent service, this correlation should be far stronger than what the evidence actually shows.

Restaurants that have experimented with eliminating tips entirely, replacing them with higher built-in wages and correspondingly higher menu prices, report mixed but generally encouraging results: more predictable income for staff, reduced friction between servers competing for the best tables, and customers who, after an initial period of adjustment, seem to accept the change without significant complaint. None of this guarantees the model would work everywhere, but it does suggest the assumption that tipping is simply the only workable system deserves considerably more skepticism than it usually receives. An industry built on such an unpredictable and, at times, discriminatory foundation is worth reconsidering, however unfamiliar the alternative might initially feel.

preguntas.enunciado: What is the author's main purpose in this text?

opciones.A: To explain the history of tipping customs around the world
opciones.B: To argue that the tipping system has serious flaws and deserves reconsideration
opciones.C: To convince readers to stop tipping servers immediately

preguntas.explicacion_correcta: El texto expone múltiples problemas del sistema de propinas y sugiere que merece ser reconsiderado, sin pedir a los clientes que dejen de dar propina de forma individual.

A.explicacion: El texto no ofrece un recuento histórico de la costumbre de dar propina.

C.explicacion: El autor no aboga por que los clientes individuales dejen de dar propina, sino por cambiar el sistema estructuralmente.

preguntas.enunciado: What can be inferred about the author's view of the claim that tipping motivates better service?

opciones.A: The author fully agrees that tipping is the best way to motivate servers
opciones.B: The author believes servers do not care about providing good service at all
opciones.C: The author doubts this claim, since the evidence supporting it is weak

preguntas.explicacion_correcta: El texto señala que la investigación que respalda esta afirmación es 'remarkably thin', mostrando duda hacia la validez del argumento.

A.explicacion: Contradice directamente la postura crítica del autor sobre esta afirmación.

B.explicacion: El autor cuestiona la relación entre propina y calidad, pero no sugiere que a los meseros no les importe el servicio.

preguntas.enunciado: What is the author's tone when discussing studies on bias in tipping?

opciones.A: concerned/critical
opciones.B: amused
opciones.C: uncertain

preguntas.explicacion_correcta: El autor presenta la evidencia sobre sesgos en las propinas de forma firme y crítica, señalándolo como un problema serio del sistema.

B.explicacion: Es un tono que no corresponde en absoluto al contenido serio de ese párrafo.

C.explicacion: El autor presenta la evidencia con firmeza, no con duda o incertidumbre.

preguntas.enunciado: How does the author seem to view restaurants that have eliminated tipping?

opciones.A: completely certain the model works everywhere
opciones.B: cautiously optimistic
opciones.C: strongly opposed to any experimentation with the tipping system

preguntas.explicacion_correcta: El autor menciona resultados 'generally encouraging' pero aclara que 'none of this guarantees the model would work everywhere', mostrando un optimismo cauteloso.

A.explicacion: El texto explícitamente aclara que no hay garantía de que el modelo funcione en todas partes.

C.explicacion: Contradice el tono positivo hacia estos experimentos, aunque el autor sí reconoce cierta cautela, no oposición.

preguntas.enunciado: What does the author imply by saying the correlation between tip size and service quality 'should be far stronger than what the evidence actually shows'?

opciones.A: That researchers have not yet studied the relationship between tips and service
opciones.B: That servers should try harder to improve their service quality
opciones.C: That the popular belief about tipping rewarding good service is not well supported by facts

preguntas.explicacion_correcta: La frase sugiere que si la propina realmente recompensara el buen servicio, la relación estadística sería mucho más fuerte de lo que realmente es.

A.explicacion: Contradice que el texto ya menciona varios estudios sobre el tema.

B.explicacion: Tergiversa el argumento, que cuestiona el sistema de propinas, no la actitud de los meseros.

texto_base:
Ask almost anyone how they manage a busy workday, and multitasking will likely come up as an essential skill, something between a talent and a badge of honor. Answering emails while sitting in a meeting, switching between multiple browser tabs, or texting a friend while reviewing a document has become so normalized that few people stop to question whether any of this actually works the way we assume it does.

The uncomfortable truth, according to a substantial body of cognitive research, is that what we call multitasking is rarely true simultaneous processing at all. Instead, the brain is rapidly switching attention back and forth between tasks, a process researchers call "task switching." Each switch carries a small but real cost in time and mental energy, as the brain must disengage from one task and reorient itself to another. These costs may seem trivial in isolation, but they accumulate rapidly across a busy day filled with constant interruptions.

Perhaps more troubling than the loss of raw efficiency is the effect multitasking appears to have on the quality of thinking itself. Deep, creative problem-solving typically requires sustained, uninterrupted attention, the kind that allows ideas to develop and connect in ways that are simply impossible when attention is constantly fragmented. Workers who multitask heavily throughout the day often report feeling busy and productive, even as objective measures of their output tell a noticeably different story.

None of this means occasional task switching is inherently harmful; checking a message during a natural pause in a task, for instance, carries a fairly minimal cost. The real danger lies in treating constant, deliberate multitasking as an admirable strategy rather than recognizing it for what cognitive science suggests it actually is: a coping mechanism for managing an overwhelming volume of demands, dressed up as a skill worth cultivating.

Some roles, of course, genuinely do require rapid attention shifts, and certain individuals may handle task switching somewhat more efficiently than others. But treating multitasking as a universally desirable skill, one that workplaces should actively encourage and employees should proudly list on a résumé, ignores a growing body of evidence suggesting we would often accomplish more, and think more clearly, by doing considerably less at once. Perhaps the most productive skill available to most workers is not the ability to juggle five tasks simultaneously, but the increasingly rare discipline of protecting focused, uninterrupted time for the one task that actually matters most in the moment.

preguntas.enunciado: What is the author's main purpose in this text?

opciones.A: To provide step-by-step instructions for multitasking more efficiently
opciones.B: To argue that all forms of task switching should be completely avoided
opciones.C: To challenge the popular belief that multitasking is an effective and desirable skill

preguntas.explicacion_correcta: El texto cuestiona repetidamente la idea popular de que la multitarea es eficiente, apoyándose en investigación cognitiva.

A.explicacion: El texto no ofrece instrucciones prácticas, sino una crítica conceptual.

B.explicacion: El autor aclara que el cambio ocasional de tareas no es dañino, solo critica el multitasking constante como estrategia deliberada.

preguntas.enunciado: What can be inferred about the author's view of workers who multitask heavily and report feeling productive?

opciones.A: The author believes their perception of productivity may not match their actual output
opciones.B: The author agrees that feeling productive always means being productive
opciones.C: The author thinks these workers are lying about how they feel

preguntas.explicacion_correcta: El texto contrasta la sensación de productividad de estos trabajadores con medidas objetivas que muestran 'a noticeably different story'.

B.explicacion: Contradice directamente el contraste que el autor establece entre percepción y medición objetiva.

C.explicacion: El texto habla de una discrepancia entre percepción y medición objetiva, no de mentiras deliberadas.

preguntas.enunciado: What is the author's tone toward the idea of listing multitasking as a skill on a résumé?

opciones.A: admiring
opciones.B: critical/ironic
opciones.C: neutral

preguntas.explicacion_correcta: La frase 'dressed up as a skill worth cultivating' revela una postura irónica y crítica hacia esa práctica.

A.explicacion: Es el tono opuesto al que realmente muestra el texto.

C.explicacion: El lenguaje empleado revela una postura claramente crítica, no neutral.

preguntas.enunciado: What does the author imply by describing multitasking as 'a coping mechanism... dressed up as a skill worth cultivating'?

opciones.A: That multitasking should be taught more explicitly in schools and workplaces
opciones.B: That workers who multitask are trying to hide how overwhelmed they feel
opciones.C: That multitasking is actually a response to being overwhelmed, not a genuinely valuable ability

preguntas.explicacion_correcta: La frase sugiere que lo que se presenta como una habilidad admirable es, en realidad, una forma de lidiar con exigencias excesivas.

A.explicacion: Contradice el tono crítico del autor hacia la multitarea como habilidad a fomentar.

B.explicacion: Se acerca al sentido real, pero enfatiza un ocultamiento intencional en vez de la reinterpretación cultural del comportamiento que describe el autor.

preguntas.enunciado: What does the author imply by calling protecting focused time an 'increasingly rare discipline'?

opciones.A: That sustained focus has become uncommon in modern work environments, even though it is valuable
opciones.B: That very few people are physically capable of concentrating on one task
opciones.C: That focused work used to be a common but now outdated method that no longer works

preguntas.explicacion_correcta: La frase sugiere que proteger el enfoque sostenido, aunque valioso, se ha vuelto poco común en los entornos laborales actuales.

B.explicacion: No se refiere a una limitación física, sino a un hábito cultural poco común.

C.explicacion: Invierte parcialmente la implicación, sugiriendo que el enfoque ya no funciona, cuando el autor en realidad lo valora como la habilidad más productiva.

texto_base:
Few aspects of school life generate as much anxiety, for students and parents alike, as the letter or number that appears at the top of a graded assignment. Grades are so thoroughly woven into the structure of modern education that questioning their value can feel almost absurd, akin to questioning whether schools should have classrooms. Yet a small but persistent group of educators has spent decades arguing that traditional grading may be doing considerably more harm than most people realize.

The central complaint against grades is not that assessment itself is unnecessary, but that reducing a student's understanding of an entire subject to a single letter or number sacrifices almost all useful information in the process. A "B" on a history essay reveals nothing about which specific skills the student has mastered and which still need work; it simply signals a vague, undifferentiated level of overall performance. Detailed feedback, by contrast, tells a student exactly what to improve, yet many students barely glance past the grade itself once one has been assigned.

There is also mounting evidence that grades can actively undermine the kind of learning schools claim to value most. Studies in educational psychology have repeatedly found that when students are graded, they become measurably more likely to choose easier tasks, avoid academic risks, and lose interest in a subject once the grade has been recorded, even if they previously found the material genuinely engaging. In other words, grades appear to shift a student's underlying motivation away from curiosity and mastery and toward the comparatively narrow goal of protecting a number.

None of this means all forms of evaluation should disappear. Students, parents, and institutions such as universities reasonably want some way to gauge progress and compare performance. But detailed, written feedback focused on specific skills, portfolios of actual student work, and narrative evaluations from teachers can often communicate far more useful information than a single letter ever could, provided schools are willing to invest the additional time such approaches typically require.

Critics of grade reform often point out, not unreasonably, that alternative systems take considerably more time for teachers to implement and can be harder for outside institutions to interpret quickly. These are legitimate practical obstacles, not reasons to dismiss the underlying critique of traditional grading altogether. The question worth asking is not whether evaluation should exist in some form, since it clearly should, but whether the specific system currently used in most schools genuinely serves students' learning, or whether it has simply persisted this long because changing deeply entrenched institutional habits is difficult, regardless of the evidence against them.

preguntas.enunciado: What is the author's main purpose in this text?

opciones.A: To argue that traditional grading may harm learning and deserves serious reconsideration
opciones.B: To provide a complete guide for teachers on how to grade fairly
opciones.C: To argue that all forms of student evaluation should be eliminated

preguntas.explicacion_correcta: El texto expone evidencia de que las calificaciones tradicionales pueden dañar el aprendizaje y propone reconsiderarlas.

B.explicacion: El texto no ofrece una guía práctica de calificación, sino una crítica conceptual del sistema.

C.explicacion: El autor aclara explícitamente que no toda evaluación debería desaparecer.

preguntas.enunciado: What can be inferred about the author's view of students who focus mainly on the grade rather than the feedback?

opciones.A: The author believes these students are simply lazy and unmotivated
opciones.B: The author sees this as a sign that grades may be shifting students' motivation away from genuine learning
opciones.C: The author thinks teachers should stop giving feedback altogether

preguntas.explicacion_correcta: El texto conecta este comportamiento con la idea de que las calificaciones desplazan la motivación hacia proteger un número, no hacia aprender.

A.explicacion: El texto no atribuye este comportamiento a la pereza, sino al efecto de las calificaciones en la motivación.

C.explicacion: Invierte la solución propuesta, ya que el autor en realidad valora la retroalimentación detallada.

preguntas.enunciado: What can be inferred about the author's opinion of critics who point out practical obstacles to grade reform?

opciones.A: The author believes these critics have no valid points at all
opciones.B: The author agrees completely and thinks grade reform should be abandoned
opciones.C: The author considers their concerns legitimate but insufficient to dismiss the broader critique of grading

preguntas.explicacion_correcta: El texto describe estas objeciones como 'legitimate practical obstacles, not reasons to dismiss the underlying critique'.

A.explicacion: Contradice que el autor reconoce explícitamente que los obstáculos prácticos son legítimos.

B.explicacion: Contradice el tono general del texto, que defiende la necesidad de reconsiderar el sistema de calificación.

preguntas.enunciado: What is the author's tone when describing how deeply grades are embedded in education?

opciones.A: somewhat critical, treating this as an unquestioned habit rather than a proven necessity
opciones.B: purely celebratory of how well the current system works
opciones.C: completely neutral, with no opinion on the matter

preguntas.explicacion_correcta: Comparar el cuestionar las calificaciones con algo 'casi absurdo' revela una crítica implícita a lo naturalizado que está el sistema.

B.explicacion: Es el tono opuesto al que realmente muestra el texto.

C.explicacion: El lenguaje utilizado muestra una postura clara, no neutralidad.

preguntas.enunciado: What does the author imply by comparing questioning grades to 'questioning whether schools should have classrooms'?

opciones.A: That classrooms and grades are equally essential and should never be changed
opciones.B: That grades are treated as an unquestionable assumption, even though they deserve scrutiny like anything else
opciones.C: That schools should reconsider whether classrooms are still necessary

preguntas.explicacion_correcta: La comparación exagerada resalta cómo las calificaciones se han naturalizado hasta el punto de parecer incuestionables, aunque no deberían estarlo.

A.explicacion: Contradice el punto del autor, que precisamente cuestiona esa naturalización.

C.explicacion: Interpreta literalmente la comparación retórica, en vez de captar su función sobre la naturalización de las calificaciones.

texto_base:
Social media platforms were built on a seemingly simple promise: connect people more easily than ever before, regardless of distance, schedule, or circumstance. By most measures, that promise has technically been fulfilled. People today can maintain contact with hundreds or even thousands of acquaintances, share updates instantly, and locate old classmates in seconds. Yet a growing body of research suggests that this technical connectivity may be coming at the cost of something harder to measure but arguably more important: genuine social closeness.

Part of the issue lies in what researchers call the difference between broad and deep social connection. Social media excels at maintaining broad networks, keeping hundreds of loose acquaintances technically within reach through likes, occasional comments, and passive updates. What it struggles to replicate is the deep, sustained attention that close relationships actually require, the kind built through uninterrupted conversation, shared physical presence, and the small, unglamorous moments that rarely make it into a curated online post.

Several studies have found a troubling pattern among heavy social media users: despite spending hours each day theoretically "connected" to others, many report feeling lonelier and more socially isolated than people who use these platforms far less. Researchers have proposed several explanations, including the tendency to compare one's own ordinary life to the carefully curated highlight reels other people choose to share, as well as the simple fact that time spent scrolling is time not spent engaged in the kind of face-to-face interaction that appears to matter most for emotional wellbeing.

None of this means social media offers no genuine social value; it can meaningfully help people maintain relationships across long distances, find communities built around niche interests, and stay informed about events in the lives of people they genuinely care about. The problem arises less from the technology itself and more from how it is often used, frequently as a substitute for deeper interaction rather than a supplement that makes deeper interaction easier to sustain.

Perhaps the most useful shift would involve treating social media the way we already treat other convenient but limited tools: genuinely useful for certain narrow purposes, but a poor substitute for the sustained, unhurried attention that real relationships have always required. Recognizing this distinction will not, by itself, solve the loneliness many heavy users report experiencing. But continuing to treat broad digital connectivity as a full substitute for deep social closeness seems, at the very least, worth questioning rather than simply accepting as an unavoidable feature of modern life.

preguntas.enunciado: What is the author's main purpose in this text?

opciones.A: To provide statistics on how many people use social media worldwide
opciones.B: To question whether social media's promise of connection truly delivers meaningful social closeness
opciones.C: To argue that social media should be banned for teenagers

preguntas.explicacion_correcta: El texto cuestiona si la conectividad técnica de las redes sociales realmente equivale a cercanía social genuina.

A.explicacion: El texto no presenta estadísticas de uso, sino un análisis conceptual.

C.explicacion: El texto no menciona ninguna prohibición ni se enfoca en adolescentes específicamente.

preguntas.enunciado: What can be inferred about the author's view of the correlation between heavy social media use and loneliness?

opciones.A: The author believes this correlation has been completely disproven by recent research
opciones.B: The author thinks loneliness causes people to use social media more, not the other way around
opciones.C: The author considers it a meaningful pattern worth taking seriously, not a coincidence

preguntas.explicacion_correcta: El texto presenta esta correlación como un 'troubling pattern' respaldado por varios estudios, tratándola como algo significativo.

A.explicacion: Contradice que el texto presenta esta correlación como respaldada por evidencia, no refutada.

B.explicacion: Invierte la dirección causal sugerida por el texto sin que esa alternativa se mencione explícitamente.

preguntas.enunciado: What is the author's tone toward social media overall?

opciones.A: completely hostile, viewing social media as entirely harmful
opciones.B: balanced but concerned, acknowledging benefits while questioning overreliance
opciones.C: uncritically enthusiastic about its benefits

preguntas.explicacion_correcta: El autor reconoce beneficios reales de las redes sociales, pero mantiene una preocupación constante sobre su uso como sustituto de la conexión profunda.

A.explicacion: El texto explícitamente reconoce beneficios genuinos de las redes sociales.

C.explicacion: Contradice el tono de preocupación que domina el texto, aunque el autor sí reconoce algunos beneficios reales.

preguntas.enunciado: What does the author imply by describing online posts as 'curated highlight reels'?

opciones.A: That most social media posts are professionally edited videos
opciones.B: That people tend to share only the best parts of their lives online, which can create unfair comparisons
opciones.C: That people should stop posting about their achievements online

preguntas.explicacion_correcta: La expresión sugiere que la gente selecciona cuidadosamente lo que comparte, generando comparaciones poco realistas con la vida cotidiana de los demás.

A.explicacion: Interpreta 'curated highlight reels' de forma literal, como edición profesional de video, en vez de en sentido figurado.

C.explicacion: Interpreta la crítica como una recomendación de comportamiento específica, en vez de una observación sobre el efecto psicológico de comparar vidas curadas.

preguntas.enunciado: Why does the author compare social media to 'other convenient but limited tools'?

opciones.A: To argue that social media is completely useless and should be avoided entirely
opciones.B: To claim that all modern technology has the exact same limitations as social media
opciones.C: To suggest social media has real, narrow benefits but should not replace deeper forms of social connection

preguntas.explicacion_correcta: La comparación busca ubicar a las redes sociales como una herramienta útil pero limitada, no como un sustituto completo de la conexión humana profunda.

A.explicacion: Contradice que el autor reconoce explícitamente beneficios genuinos de las redes sociales.

B.explicacion: Generaliza la comparación específica hecha por el autor a toda la tecnología, lo cual no se afirma en el texto.
